raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that recursively removes all *.txt in /system and auto-yes. Use find with -delete or rm -r. To answer y to any prompt, use rm -f or find -delete. So: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -delete. That's it.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command that recursively removes all *.txt in /system and auto-yes. Use find with -delete or rm -r. To answer y to any prompt, use rm -f or find -delete. So: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -delete. That's it.
